mnemonics.title: Injectivity Check
mnemonics.content: IIP: Imagine Injectivity as 'Input must be Identical for same Output Picture' - if two inputs give same output, they must be the same input.

mnemonics.title: Surjectivity Check
mnemonics.content: SUPER: Surjectivity means 'Some Unique Person (input) Enjoys each Result (output)' - every output has at least one input partner.

mnemonics.title: Bijective = One-to-One + Onto
mnemonics.content: BIO: Bijective = B-IO. B for Both, IO for Injective Onto. Or remember: A bijective function is 'by-jective' - it takes all possible values!

mnemonics.title: Inverse Function
mnemonics.content: INVERSE: If f sends x→y, then f⁻¹ sends y→x. Think 'INVERSE = INPUT REVERSES': swap domain and codomain direction.

acronyms.usage: Set of all possible x values for which f(x) is defined
Set of all possible output values specified for the function
Subset of codomain consisting of actual output values f(x)
Function that is both one-to-one and onto has an inverse

visualTips.visualization: Draw input x going into a 'function machine' that transforms it to output y. For composition, connect output of g-machine to input of f-machine.
Horizontal line test: If any horizontal line crosses the graph more than once, function is not injective. For sets, draw arrows - no two arrows should point to same element.
All elements in codomain must be 'hit' by at least one arrow from domain. Every element in the right circle must have at least one incoming arrow.
Reflect the function's arrow diagram across a diagonal line. If the reflection is also a function, then inverse exists (bijective case).

analogies.analogy: Like passing output of one assembly line as input to next. First machine g processes x, then its output feeds into f for final product.
Like a reversible chemical reaction. If forward reaction converts A→B, inverse converts B→A. Only works if reaction is complete and reversible (bijective).
Like a perfect marriage matchmaking: every person (domain) gets exactly one partner (codomain), and everyone in the dating pool gets matched.
Like a music band taking photos: each band member gets a unique pose (injective), but some photo frames remain empty (not every codomain element used).
